title: hex Context Injection into opencode
description: Enable opencode to leverage hex's complete ecosystem (MCP tools, agent definitions, skills, hooks, enforcement rules) via opencode.json configuration

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject injects all hex context into opencode.json
given: opencode.json may or may not exist at project root or ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json
then: $schema field is set to 'https://opencode.ai/config.json'
hex MCP server entry is added to mcp key with type: 'local', command: ['hex', 'mcp'], enabled: true
HEX_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable is set to '.' in MCP entry
hex agent definitions are added to hex.context.agents
hex skills are added to hex.context.skills
hex hooks are added to hex.context.hooks
hex enforcement rules are added to hex.context.enforcement
existing opencode.json config is preserved via non-destructive merge
original config is backed up before modification
success message is displayed with config file location

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject --mcp injects only MCP server spec
then: hex MCP entry is added to mcp key: type 'local', command ['hex', 'mcp'], enabled true
other hex context (agents, skills, hooks) is NOT modified
existing MCP server configurations are preserved

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject --agents injects only agent definitions
then: hex agent definitions (YAML) are read from embedded assets
agents are added to hex.context.agents in opencode.json
14+ agents (feature-developer, planner, hex-coder, integrator, etc.) become available

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject --skills injects only skills
then: hex skills are read from embedded assets
28+ skills are added to hex.context.skills in opencode.json

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject --hooks injects only hooks
then: hex hooks are read from embedded helpers/*.cjs assets
hooks are added to hex.context.hooks in opencode.json

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject --enforcement injects only enforcement rules
then: ADR compliance rules are read from .hex/adr-rules.toml
rules are added to hex.context.enforcement in opencode.json

behaviors.description: hex opencode inject --global writes to global config
then: config is written to ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json (not project-local)
HEX_PROJECT_ROOT is set to the absolute path of the current directory
parent directories are created if needed

behaviors.description: hex opencode detects opencode.json config location
when: user runs any hex opencode command
then: hex checks ./opencode.json first (project root, highest precedence)
if not found, checks ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json (global)
for inject without --global, defaults to creating ./opencode.json if not found

behaviors.description: hex opencode uses non-destructive merge with serde flatten
then: existing $schema field is preserved
existing mcp entries (other than hex) are preserved
existing hex.context is updated, not replaced
unknown top-level keys are preserved via serde flatten
no user config is lost during round-trip

behaviors.description: hex opencode backs up config before modification
when: user runs hex opencode inject or hex opencode remove
then: original config is copied to opencode.json.backup.<timestamp>
unix timestamp is used in backup filename

behaviors.description: hex opencode remove removes all hex context from opencode.json
given: hex context was previously injected into opencode.json
then: hex entry is removed from mcp key
hex context block is removed
other opencode configuration (other mcp servers, unknown keys) is preserved
$schema field is preserved

behaviors.description: hex opencode disable sets enabled: false on hex MCP server
given: hex MCP server is configured in opencode.json
then: mcp.hex.enabled is set to false
all other config (command, environment, hex context) is preserved
config is backed up before modification

behaviors.description: hex opencode enable sets enabled: true on hex MCP server
given: hex MCP server is configured in opencode.json with enabled: false
then: mcp.hex.enabled is set to true
all other config is preserved

behaviors.description: hex opencode disable errors when hex MCP not configured
given: hex MCP server is NOT configured in opencode.json
then: error message indicates hex MCP server not configured
suggests running hex opencode inject --mcp first

behaviors.description: hex opencode status shows configured hex context
given: hex may or may not be configured in opencode
then: displays config file location
displays whether hex MCP server is enabled or disabled
displays MCP command and environment variables
displays hex version from hex.version
displays agent, skill, hook counts from hex.context
displays whether enforcement is configured

behaviors.description: hex opencode status handles missing config
then: displays 'Config: not found'
suggests running hex opencode inject

behaviors.description: hex opencode handles corrupted config file
given: opencode.json exists but is invalid JSON
then: error message indicates config is corrupted
original file is not modified

behaviors.description: hex MCP server is injected with correct opencode specification
when: MCP server spec is being injected
then: type field is set to 'local'
command field is set to ['hex', 'mcp'] (array, not string)
enabled field is set to true
environment.HEX_PROJECT_ROOT is set to '.' (project-local) or absolute path (global)

behaviors.description: hex agent definitions are correctly loaded from embedded assets
given: hex agent YAML definitions are embedded in hex-cli binary
then: agent definitions are read from embedded assets/agents/*.yaml
each agent's name and content are extracted
agents are stored in hex.context.agents array

non_behaviors.description: hex opencode never deletes existing opencode configuration
then: existing mcp entries, unknown keys, and $schema are preserved via serde flatten

non_behaviors.description: hex opencode never modifies hex's own configuration files
then: hex's agents/, skills/, helpers/, .hex/ directories are not modified

non_behaviors.description: hex opencode never uses shell string for MCP command
given: hex MCP entry is being created
then: command is always an array ['hex', 'mcp'], never a shell string

properties.property: After hex opencode inject, opencode.json remains valid JSON with $schema
Running hex opencode inject twice does not duplicate MCP server
properties.theorem: config['mcp']['hex'] exists exactly once after two inject calls

properties.property: hex opencode remove restores original config (minus hex entries)
properties.theorem: After inject → remove, all non-hex config keys equal original
For any key K not in {$schema, mcp, hex}, config[K] is unchanged after inject
Running disable twice yields same state as running it once
